analysis.false_positive_analysis: Unable to determine false positive likelihood.

raw_response.summary: The target network appears to have several vulnerabilities, including exposed FTP credentials, unencrypted HTTP traffic, and outdated software versions.

findings.vulnerability: FTP sends credentials in plain text. Vulnerable to sniffing.
findings.exploitation: An attacker can intercept and steal FTP credentials, potentially gaining unauthorized access to sensitive data.
findings.remediation: Implement a secure authentication mechanism, such as SSL/TLS encryption, and consider using a more secure FTP server software.

findings.vulnerability: HTTP is unencrypted. Susceptible to Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) attacks.
findings.exploitation: An attacker can intercept and modify HTTP traffic, potentially injecting malicious scripts or stealing sensitive data.
findings.remediation: Implement HTTPS encryption and ensure that all web traffic is redirected to the secure protocol.

findings.vulnerability: Ensure this service is intentionally exposed and properly secured.
findings.exploitation: An attacker can exploit vulnerabilities in the POP3 service to gain unauthorized access to email accounts.
findings.remediation: Implement secure authentication mechanisms and consider using a more secure email server software.

findings.vulnerability: Ensure this service is intentionally exposed and properly secured.
findings.exploitation: An attacker can exploit vulnerabilities in the IMAP service to gain unauthorized access to email accounts.
findings.remediation: Implement secure authentication mechanisms and consider using a more secure email server software.
